title: anagallis arvensis subsp. micrantha
intro: Anagallis arvensis subsp. micrantha, also known as small blue-pimpernel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Anagallis arvensis subsp. micrantha include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve for topical use, and is also used in herbal remedies to treat various ailments.
